paper_title: HySparse: A Hybrid Sparse Attention Architecture with Oracle Token Selection and KV Cache Sharing

architecture.name: Hybrid Sparse Attention (HySparse)
architecture.key_idea: Interleave every full attention layer with multiple sparse attention layers

design.full_attention_layers: Precisely identify important token selection and produce KV caches
design.sparse_attention_layers: Derive important token selection and KV caches from preceding full layer
design.swa_branch: Each sparse attention layer is augmented with an additional SWA branch that maintains a small, local KV cache

problems_addressed: Proxy-based token selection is approximate and may fail to capture true token importance
Computation reduction without memory relief in dynamic sparse attention methods

experimental_setup.final_layer: Employs full attention to preserve global aggregation

results.performance: Consistently outperforms both full attention and hybrid SWA baselines
results.kv_cache: No additional KV cache cost relative to hybrid SWA baseline

efficiency.80B_MoE_model: With 49 total layers, only 5 layers use full attention
efficiency.kv_reduction: Nearly 10× KV cache reduction while maintaining substantial performance gains

results.comparison: Can significantly reduce the number of full attention layers compared to Hybrid SWA

title: HySparse: A Hybrid Sparse Attention Architecture with Oracle Token Selection and KV Cache Sharing
